sentences.sentence: Alan Macfarlane, professor of anthropological science at King's College, Cambridge, has, like other historians, spent decades wrestling with the enigma of the Industrial Revolution.
sentences.translation: 剑桥大学国王学院人类学科学教授艾伦·麦克法兰和其他历史学家一样，花了几十年时间试图解开工业革命之谜。

structure.text: professor of anthropological science at King's College, Cambridge
has, like other historians, spent decades wrestling with the enigma of the Industrial Revolution

difficult_points: 两个逗号之间的 professor... 是 Alan Macfarlane 的同位语。
spend time doing 表示花时间做某事，wrestling with 在此表示努力解决难题。

sentences.sentence: Why did this particular Big Bang – the world-changing birth of industry – happen in Britain?
sentences.translation: 为什么这场特殊的“大爆炸”——改变世界的工业诞生——会发生在英国？

structure.text: did this particular Big Bang – the world-changing birth of industry – happen in Britain

difficult_points: 破折号之间的内容解释 Big Bang 在此指工业的诞生。
did 提前构成特殊疑问句，实义动词 happen 使用原形。

sentences.sentence: And why did it strike at the end of the 18th century?
sentences.translation: 而它为什么偏偏在18世纪末爆发？

structure.text: at the end of the 18th century

difficult_points: it 指前一句的工业革命。
strike 在这里不是“击打”，而是指重大变化突然发生。

sentences.sentence: Macfarlane compares the puzzle to a combination lock.
sentences.translation: 麦克法兰把这个谜题比作一把密码锁。
difficult_points: the puzzle 指工业革命为何在特定时间发生于英国。
compare A to B 表示把 A 比作 B。

sentences.sentence: 'There are about 20 different factors and all of them need to be present before the revolution can happen,' he says.
sentences.translation: 他说：“大约有20个不同因素，而且在革命发生之前，这些因素必须全部具备。”

structure.text: and all of them need to be present

difficult_points: all of them 指前面的约20个因素。
be present 在此表示条件“具备”，不是“在场”。

sentences.sentence: For industry to take off, there needs to be the technology and power to drive factories, large urban populations to provide cheap labour, easy transport to move goods around, an affluent middle-class willing to buy mass-produced objects, a market-driven economy and a political system that allows this to happen.
sentences.translation: 要让工业腾飞，必须具备驱动工厂的技术和动力、提供廉价劳动力的大量城市人口、运输货物的便捷交通、愿意购买批量生产商品的富裕中产阶级、市场驱动型经济，以及允许这一切发生的政治制度。

structure.text: the technology and power to drive factories, large urban populations to provide cheap labour, easy transport to move goods around, an affluent middle-class willing to buy mass-produced objects, a market-driven economy and a political system

difficult_points: For industry to take off 是“for＋名词＋不定式”结构，表示要实现的目标。
there needs to be 后面是六项并列条件，多个不定式和分词分别后置修饰对应名词。
that 指 a political system，this 指前面列出的工业化条件发挥作用。

sentences.sentence: While this was the case for England, other nations, such as Japan, the Netherlands and France also met some of these criteria but were not industrialising.
sentences.translation: 英格兰确实具备这些条件，而日本、荷兰和法国等其他国家也符合其中一些标准，却没有走向工业化。

structure.text: While this was the case for England
such as Japan, the Netherlands and France
also met some of these criteria but were not industrialising

difficult_points: While 在此表示对比，相当于“虽然、而”。
this 指上一句列出的工业化所需条件。
met 与 were not industrialising 是由 but 连接的并列谓语。

sentences.sentence: 'All these factors must have been necessary but not sufficient to cause the revolution,' says Macfarlane.
sentences.translation: 麦克法兰说：“所有这些因素必定都是必要条件，但不足以引发工业革命。”
difficult_points: must have been 表示对过去情况的有把握推断。
necessary but not sufficient 是逻辑表达，指必要但不充分。

sentences.sentence: 'After all, Holland had everything except coal, while China also had many of these factors.
sentences.translation: “毕竟，荷兰除了煤以外什么条件都有，而中国也具备其中许多因素。

structure.text: while China also had many of these factors

difficult_points: After all 引出支持前述判断的证据。
while 表示荷兰和中国情况的并列对照；引号将在下一句结束。

sentences.sentence: Most historians are convinced there are one or two missing factors that you need to open the lock.'
sentences.translation: 大多数历史学家确信，还缺少一两个打开这把锁所必需的因素。”

structure.text: there are one or two missing factors
that you need to open the lock

difficult_points: are convinced 后省略 that，后面的存在句是确信的内容。
that 修饰 missing factors，open the lock 延续工业革命如密码锁的比喻。

sentences.sentence: The missing factors, he proposes, are to be found in almost every kitchen cupboard.
sentences.translation: 他提出，缺失的因素几乎在每个厨房橱柜里都能找到。
difficult_points: he proposes 是插入的报告语，不影响主句结构。
are to be found 在此表示“可以找到、存在于”。

sentences.sentence: Tea and beer, two of the nation`s favourite drinks, fuelled the revolution.
sentences.translation: 茶和啤酒这两种英国人最喜爱的饮品推动了工业革命。

structure.text: two of the nation`s favourite drinks

difficult_points: two of... 是 Tea and beer 的同位语。
fuelled 是比喻用法，表示为工业革命提供动力、促使其发展。

sentences.sentence: The antiseptic properties of tannin, the active ingredient in tea, and of hops in beer – plus the fact that both are made with boiled water – allowed urban communities to flourish at close quarters without succumbing to water-borne diseases such as dysentery.
sentences.translation: 茶中活性成分单宁和啤酒花的抗菌特性，再加上两种饮品都用沸水制作，使城市社区即使人口密集也能兴旺发展，而不至于遭受痢疾等水传播疾病。

structure.text: The antiseptic properties of tannin, the active ingredient in tea, and of hops in beer
plus the fact that both are made with boiled water
that both are made with boiled water
allowed urban communities to flourish at close quarters
without succumbing to water-borne diseases such as dysentery

difficult_points: 主语由茶中单宁和啤酒花的抗菌特性构成，破折号间又补充沸水这一因素。
both 指 tea and beer；allow A to do 表示这些因素使城市社区得以发展。
without succumbing to... 表示没有因水传播疾病而患病或衰败。

sentences.sentence: The theory sounds eccentric but once he starts to explain the detective work that went into his deduction, the skepticism gives way to wary admiration.
sentences.translation: 这套理论听起来古怪，但当他开始解释形成这一推论所做的侦探式研究时，怀疑便逐渐转为谨慎的赞赏。

structure.text: but once he starts to explain the detective work
the skepticism gives way to wary admiration

difficult_points: once 引导时间状语从句，表示“一旦、当……之后”。
that 修饰 detective work，go into 表示为得出推论所投入的工作。
give way to 表示一种态度逐渐被另一种态度取代。

expressions.meaning_zh: 投入到推论形成过程中

sentences.sentence: Macfarlane's case has been strengthened by support from notable quarters – Roy Porter, the distinguished medical historian, recently wrote a favourable appraisal of his research.
sentences.translation: 来自权威人士的支持增强了麦克法兰论点的说服力——著名医学史学家罗伊·波特最近对他的研究作出了积极评价。

structure.text: has been strengthened by support from notable quarters
recently wrote a favourable appraisal of his research

difficult_points: case 在此指麦克法兰提出的论点和论证，不是案件。
破折号后的完整句子举例说明 support from notable quarters。

sentences.sentence: Macfarlane had wondered for a long time how the Industrial Revolution came about.
sentences.translation: 麦克法兰长期以来一直在思考工业革命究竟是如何发生的。

structure.text: had wondered for a long time
how the Industrial Revolution came about

difficult_points: how 从句作 wondered 的宾语，使用陈述语序。
come about 表示某事如何发生或形成。

sentences.sentence: Historians had alighted on one interesting factor around the mid-18th century that required explanation.
sentences.translation: 历史学家注意到18世纪中叶前后的一个有趣因素，而这一因素需要得到解释。

structure.text: one interesting factor around the mid-18th century

difficult_points: alight on 在此表示偶然发现或注意到。
that 指 one interesting factor，不是 the mid-18th century。

sentences.sentence: Between about 1650 and 1740, the population in Britain was static.
sentences.translation: 大约1650年至1740年间，英国人口保持不变。
difficult_points: static 在人口语境中表示总量没有明显增长。
Between A and B 明确限定统计所指的时期。

sentences.sentence: But then there was a burst in population growth.
sentences.translation: 但随后人口突然快速增长。
difficult_points: there was 是存在句，信息中心是 a burst。
a burst 表示短时间内突然出现的大幅增长。

sentences.sentence: Macfarlane says: 'The infant mortality rate halved in the space of 20 years, and this happened in both rural areas and cities, and across all classes.
sentences.translation: 麦克法兰说：“婴儿死亡率在短短20年内减半，而且这一变化同时出现在农村和城市，遍及所有社会阶层。

structure.text: The infant mortality rate halved in the space of 20 years
and this happened in both rural areas and cities, and across all classes

difficult_points: this 指婴儿死亡率在20年内减半这一变化。
both...and... 连接农村和城市，across all classes 又补充其社会覆盖面；引语尚未结束。

sentences.sentence: People suggested four possible causes.
sentences.translation: 人们提出了四种可能的原因。
difficult_points: suggest 在此表示提出一种解释，而非建议采取行动。
four possible causes 引出后续逐一排查的原因。

sentences.sentence: Was there a sudden change in the viruses and bacteria around?
sentences.translation: 周围的病毒和细菌是否突然发生了变化？

structure.text: a sudden change in the viruses and bacteria around

difficult_points: 这是 there be 结构的一般疑问句，be 动词移到 there 前。
around 作后置修饰，指当时环境中存在的病毒和细菌。

sentences.sentence: Unlikely.
sentences.translation: 不太可能。
difficult_points: 这是省略句，完整含义是 Such a change was unlikely。
单独成句用于迅速否定前一个假设。

sentences.sentence: Was there a revolution in medical science?
sentences.translation: 医学是否发生了一场革命？
difficult_points: 这是第二个被检验的原因，同样使用存在句疑问结构。
revolution 在此表示医学领域的根本性突破。

sentences.sentence: But this was a century before Lister's revolution*.
sentences.translation: 但这比李斯特带来的医学革命早了整整一个世纪。
difficult_points: this 指婴儿死亡率下降发生的时期。
before 后用历史事件作时间参照，说明医学革命无法解释此前的变化。

sentences.sentence: Was there a change in environmental conditions?
sentences.translation: 环境条件是否发生了变化？
difficult_points: 这是提出的第三个可能原因。
environmental conditions 泛指影响健康的生活与自然环境。

sentences.sentence: There were improvements in agriculture that wiped out malaria, but these were small gains.
sentences.translation: 农业方面的改进确实消除了疟疾，但这些只是有限的进步。
difficult_points: that 指 improvements in agriculture，说明这些改进消除了疟疾。
these 同样指农业改进，small gains 表示其总体影响有限。

sentences.sentence: Sanitation did not become widespread until the 19th century.
sentences.translation: 卫生设施直到19世纪才普及。
difficult_points: not...until... 表示“直到……才……”。
该时间晚于婴儿死亡率下降，因而卫生改善也不能解释这一现象。

sentences.sentence: The only option left is food.
sentences.translation: 剩下的唯一解释就是食物。
difficult_points: left 是过去分词，后置修饰 option，表示排除其他可能后剩下的选项。
food 在此代表饮食与营养状况这一可能原因。

sentences.sentence: But the height and weight statistics show a decline.
sentences.translation: 但身高和体重统计数据却显示出下降趋势。

structure.text: But the height and weight statistics

difficult_points: But 表明统计证据与食物改善的假设相反。
a decline 指人们的身高和体重下降，暗示营养状况并未改善。

sentences.sentence: So the food must have got worse.
sentences.translation: 因此，食物状况必定是变差了。
difficult_points: must have got 表示根据证据对过去作出的强推断。
worse 与前一句身高体重下降形成因果解释。

sentences.sentence: Efforts to explain this sudden reduction in child deaths appeared to draw a blank.'
sentences.translation: 解释儿童死亡人数骤减的种种努力似乎都没有找到答案。”

structure.text: Efforts to explain this sudden reduction in child deaths
to explain this sudden reduction in child deaths

difficult_points: to explain... 修饰 Efforts，说明这些努力的目标。
draw a blank 是习语，表示未能找到答案或取得结果；句末引号结束长段引语。

sentences.sentence: This population burst seemed to happen at just right time to provide labour for the Industrial Revolution.
sentences.translation: 这次人口激增似乎恰好发生在能够为工业革命提供劳动力的时刻。

structure.text: to provide labour for the Industrial Revolution

difficult_points: This population burst 指前文18世纪中叶人口突然增长。
to provide labour... 是结果或作用性不定式，说明人口增长与工业革命用工需求的契合。

sentences.sentence: 'When you start moving towards an industrial revolution, it is economically efficient to have people living close together,' says Macfarlane.
sentences.translation: 麦克法兰说：“当一个社会开始迈向工业革命时，让人们集中居住在经济上更有效率。”

structure.text: When you start moving towards an industrial revolution
to have people living close together

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，真正的主语是 to have people living close together。
have people living 是“have＋宾语＋现在分词”，表示让人们处于集中居住的状态。

sentences.sentence: 'But then you get disease, particularly from human waste.'
sentences.translation: “但这样一来就会出现疾病，尤其是由人类排泄物造成的疾病。”
difficult_points: you 是泛指，指人口密集的社会，而非具体读者。
from human waste 说明疾病的主要来源。

sentences.sentence: Some digging around in historical records revealed that there was a change in the incidence of water-borne disease at that time, especially dysentery.
sentences.translation: 对历史记录的一番查找显示，当时水传播疾病的发病率发生了变化，尤其是痢疾。

structure.text: Some digging around in historical records
that there was a change in the incidence of water-borne disease at that time

difficult_points: digging around 是非正式表达，指在资料中仔细搜寻。
that 从句是 revealed 的宾语，incidence 表示疾病发生率。
especially dysentery 具体指出所发现的主要水传播疾病。

expressions.meaning_zh: 在历史记录中仔细查找

sentences.sentence: Macfarlane deduced that whatever the British were drinking must have been important in regulating disease.
sentences.translation: 麦克法兰由此推断，英国人当时饮用的东西必定在控制疾病方面发挥了重要作用。

structure.text: that whatever the British were drinking must have been important in regulating disease

difficult_points: whatever 从句在 that 从句中作主语，表示“英国人所喝的任何东西”。
must have been 表示对过去情况的强推断。

sentences.sentence: He says, 'We drank beer.
sentences.translation: 他说：“我们当时喝啤酒。
difficult_points: He 指 Macfarlane，后面开始一段直接引语。
句末只有句号而没有右引号，因为引语延续到后面的句子。

sentences.sentence: For a long time, the English were protected by the strong antibacterial agent in hops, which were added to help preserve the beer.
sentences.translation: 长期以来，英国人受到啤酒花中强效抗菌成分的保护；加入啤酒花是为了帮助保存啤酒。

structure.text: the English were protected by the strong antibacterial agent in hops

difficult_points: which 指 hops，而不是 antibacterial agent，因此谓语使用复数 were。
to help preserve... 是目的不定式，说明加入啤酒花的用途。

sentences.sentence: But in the late 17th century a tax was introduced on malt, the basic ingredient of beer.
sentences.translation: 但在17世纪后期，政府开始对麦芽征税；麦芽是啤酒的基本原料。
difficult_points: was introduced on 表示开始对某种商品征收税款。
the basic ingredient of beer 是 malt 的同位语。

sentences.sentence: The poor turned to water and gin and in the 1720s the mortality rate began to rise again.
sentences.translation: 穷人于是转而饮用水和杜松子酒，到了18世纪20年代，死亡率又开始上升。

structure.text: The poor turned to water and gin
and in the 1720s the mortality rate began to rise again

difficult_points: the poor 是“the＋形容词”结构，泛指穷人群体。
turn to 在此表示因啤酒变贵而改用其他饮品。

sentences.sentence: Then it suddenly dropped again.
sentences.translation: 随后，死亡率又突然下降了。
difficult_points: it 指前一句的 mortality rate。
again 表示死亡率在此前下降、回升后再次下降。

sentences.sentence: What caused this?'
sentences.translation: 是什么导致了这一变化？”
difficult_points: What 在疑问句中直接作主语，因此不需要助动词 did。
this 指死亡率再次突然下降；句末右引号结束麦克法兰的引语。

sentences.sentence: Macfarlane looked to Japan, which was also developing large cities about the same time, and also had no sanitation.
sentences.translation: 麦克法兰把目光转向日本；日本大约在同一时期也在发展大城市，而且同样没有卫生设施。

structure.text: which was also developing large cities about the same time

difficult_points: look to 在此表示转向日本寻找比较和答案。
which 指 Japan，其后的两个谓语共同描述日本当时的情况。

sentences.sentence: Water-borne diseases had a much looser grip on the Japanese population than those in Britain.
sentences.translation: 与英国相比，水传播疾病对日本人口的影响要小得多。

structure.text: had a much looser grip on

difficult_points: have a grip on 表示对某群体有强烈影响或控制。
those 指 water-borne diseases，避免重复该名词。

sentences.sentence: Could it be the prevalence of tea in their culture?
sentences.translation: 原因会不会是饮茶在日本文化中的普及？

structure.text: the prevalence of tea in their culture

difficult_points: it 指日本水传播疾病影响较小这一现象的原因。
their 指 the Japanese population，prevalence 表示广泛存在和普及。

sentences.sentence: Macfarlane then noted that the history of tea in Britain provided an extraordinary coincidence of dates.
sentences.translation: 麦克法兰随后注意到，英国饮茶史呈现出时间上的惊人巧合。

structure.text: that the history of tea in Britain provided an extraordinary coincidence of dates

difficult_points: that 从句是 noted 的宾语，说明他注意到的内容。
coincidence of dates 指饮茶普及与婴儿死亡率下降在时间上重合。

sentences.sentence: Tea was relatively expensive until Britain started a direct clipper trade with China in the early 18th century.
sentences.translation: 茶叶原本相对昂贵，直到18世纪初英国开始通过快速帆船与中国进行直接贸易，情况才改变。

structure.text: until Britain started a direct clipper trade with China in the early 18th century

difficult_points: until 引导时间从句，说明茶价较高的状态持续到何时。
clipper trade 指利用快速帆船开展的贸易。

sentences.sentence: By the 1740s, about the time that infant mortality was dipping, the drink was common.
sentences.translation: 到18世纪40年代，也就是婴儿死亡率正在下降的时期，茶已经十分普及。

structure.text: about the time that infant mortality was dipping

difficult_points: the drink 指 tea。
about the time... 补充说明18世纪40年代恰逢婴儿死亡率下降。
was dipping 使用过去进行时，强调当时正在下降的趋势。

sentences.sentence: Macfarlane guessed that the fact that water had to be boiled, together with the stomach-purifying properties of tea meant that the breast milk provided by mothers was healthier than it had ever been.
sentences.translation: 麦克法兰推测，饮茶必须烧开水这一事实，再加上茶具有净化肠胃的特性，意味着母亲提供的母乳比以往任何时候都更健康。

structure.text: that the fact that water had to be boiled, together with the stomach-purifying properties of tea meant
that water had to be boiled
that the breast milk provided by mothers was healthier than it had ever been

difficult_points: guessed 后的宾语从句中，主语核心是 the fact，together with... 是附加成分。
第一个内层 that 说明 fact 的内容，第二个内层 that 引出 meant 的宾语。
it 指 breast milk，than it had ever been 表示比此前任何时期都更健康。

expressions.text: healthier than it had ever been
expressions.meaning_zh: 比以往任何时候都更健康

sentences.sentence: No other European nation sipped tea like the British, which, by Macfarlane's logic, pushed these other countries out of contention for the revolution.
sentences.translation: 其他欧洲国家都不像英国人那样饮茶；按照麦克法兰的逻辑，这使它们失去了成为工业革命发源地的竞争资格。

structure.text: which, by Macfarlane's logic, pushed these other countries out of contention for the revolution

difficult_points: the British 后省略了 sipped tea，即“不像英国人那样饮茶”。
which 指前面整件事，而非单独指 the British。
push...out of contention 表示使其他国家失去竞争资格。

sentences.sentence: But, if tea is a factor in the combination lock, why didn't Japan forge ahead in a tea-soaked industrial revolution of its own?
sentences.translation: 但是，如果茶是这把“密码锁”中的一个因素，为什么日本没有凭借自身浓厚的茶文化率先推进工业革命呢？

structure.text: if tea is a factor in the combination lock
in a tea-soaked industrial revolution of its own

difficult_points: if 从句沿用工业革命条件如密码锁的比喻。
forge ahead 表示迅速领先发展；tea-soaked 形象地表示深受茶文化影响。
of its own 表示日本自身的工业革命。

sentences.sentence: Macfarlane notes that even though 17th-century Japan had large cities, high literacy rates, even a futures market, it had turned its back on the essence of any work-based revolution by giving up labour-saving devices such as animals, afraid that they would put people out of work.
sentences.translation: 麦克法兰指出，尽管17世纪的日本拥有大城市、较高的识字率，甚至还有期货市场，但它因担心动物等节省劳力的工具会使人失业而放弃了这些工具，从而背离了任何以劳动生产为基础的革命核心。

structure.text: that even though 17th-century Japan had large cities, high literacy rates, even a futures market, it had turned its back on the essence of any work-based revolution
even though 17th-century Japan had large cities, high literacy rates, even a futures market
by giving up labour-saving devices such as animals
afraid that they would put people out of work
that they would put people out of work

difficult_points: even though 从句列出日本具备的先进条件，与主句的 turned its back on 形成让步关系。
by giving up... 说明日本以何种方式背离劳动革命，animals 在此被视为节省人力的工具。
afraid... 的逻辑主语是日本人，they 指 labour-saving devices。

sentences.sentence: So, the nation that we now think of as one of the most technologically advanced entered the 19th century having 'abandoned the wheel'.
sentences.translation: 因此，这个如今被我们视为科技最先进国家之一的国家，在迈入19世纪时却已经“放弃了车轮”。

structure.text: that we now think of as one of the most technologically advanced

difficult_points: that 指 the nation，think of A as B 表示把 A 视为 B。
having abandoned 使用完成式分词，表示放弃节省劳力的技术发生在迈入19世纪之前。
abandoned the wheel 是概括性比喻，指拒绝提高生产效率的工具。
